set_fields.description: Move tau-managed execution behavior onto a single typed control-plane model shared by Ralph loops, autoresearch segments, and spawned agents while removing main-session prompt modes. Main sessions should use Pi's normal selected model/thinking and Tau-owned sandbox/tool policy; specialized behavior should be delegated through named subagents such as smart, deep, and rush.
set_fields.design: Remove prompt modes as a user-facing and persisted concept: delete the /mode command, mode prompt injection, mode selector state, mode footer label, and promptModes settings namespace. Keep an execution profile only as an explicit contract for loops and spawned agents: model, thinking, tool policy, sandbox/capability data, and system prompt source where needed. Convert smart/deep/rush from virtual mode agents into normal spawnable agent definitions/configuration. Delete plan as a mode and virtual agent. Ralph and autoresearch should pin concrete execution contracts rather than mode selectors. Pi remains the runtime kernel for model selection, session UI, tool execution, auth, and model inventory.
set_fields.acceptance_criteria: Tau has no /mode command or main-session prompt-mode injection. Tau persisted state and settings no longer include promptModes, selector.mode, modelsByMode, or mode-specific footer output. smart, deep, and rush remain spawnable subagents with explicit agent definitions and configurable model/thinking/tools/spawns through the agent configuration path. plan is removed as a prompt mode and virtual agent. Ralph and autoresearch execution contracts store and apply concrete model/thinking/tool policy data without depending on a mode selector. extensions/tau gate passes.
